business.headline: Infosys grants Rs 51.75 crore ESOPs to CEO, employees still await pay hikeThe ESOP approval comes after a strong quarterly performance by the company. Infosys reported a 20.9% rise in net profit to Rs 8,501 crore in the March quarter, while revenue grew 13.4%.

Explained: How Rs 5 lakh PF withdrawal after 3 years could cost you Rs 1.5 lakhWithdrawing your PF after switching jobs may seem like an easy financial move. But what many don't realise is that an early withdrawal can come with a surprisingly high tax cost, cutting a big chunk from your savings.
Why HCLTech shares are under pressure againThe fresh decline comes just days after the company's latest quarterly results disappointed the Street and triggered a sharp sell-off in the stock.

Received money, mutual funds or shares as gifts? Don't miss these ITR rulesGifts in the form of money or investments are quite common, but they come with tax rules that are often overlooked. Knowing what to declare in your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices later.

Infosys shares down 5%, hit 52-week low: Why IT stock tumbled after Q4 resultsAt around 11:01 am, Infosys shares were trading at Rs 1,178.20 on the NSE, down 5.03% or Rs 62.40 for the day. The fall made it one of the worst performers on benchmark indices as the IT pack remained under pressure.

8th Pay Commission: Can minimum basic pay rise to Rs 72,000? Here's the latest buzzSo where did Rs 72,000 figure come from? The figure appears to stem from projected salary calculations and speculative estimates tied to revised pay matrix levels under different fitment factor scenarios.

Warner Bros investors approve Paramount's $111-billion takeoverShareholders of Warner Bros Discovery have approved its proposed merger with Paramount Global, clearing a major hurdle for the USD-111 billion deal. The transaction, including debt, would create a media giant and significantly reshape the Hollywood entertainment landscape.

Using UPI daily? Here's why your monthly budget feels tighterFrom groceries to coffee, UPI is now part of daily life. But while each payment feels minor, the total can add up faster than expected, leaving many wondering where their money really went.

Between records and reality: What a special inspection revealed about labour law gapsIn the fourth episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how a special inspection exposed the gap between official compliance records, weak enforcement and workplace reality.

entertainment.headline: Context matters: Rakul Preet rejects situationship narrative after Jackky's remark Actor Rakul Preet Singh responded after husband Jackky Bhagnani's "situationship" remark about their marriage sparked debate online. Calling it taken out of context, she explained what he really meant about their marriage and why nuance matters.

Anupam Kher recalls meeting Michael Jackson: It felt like touching magic Actor Anupam Kher recalled his memory of meeting Michael Jackson in 1996 during his India trip. Kher, in a heartwarming note, described it as a surreal experience.

Rakesh Bedi on Rs 1 crore Dhurandhar 2 bonus rumours: Hasn't reached my account Rakesh Bedi reacted to rumours claiming he received a Rs 1 crore bonus after the success of Dhurandhar 2 and said no such amount had reached him. The actor responded to the reports with humour while addressing the speculation.

Ahaan Panday, Aneet Padda hide faces, dodge paps at Amritsar airport. Watch Actors Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda were clicked avoiding paparazzi at the airport. The two covered their faces and maintained a low profile in a viral video.

Lawrence of Punjab: Centre halts OTT release of gangster docuseries after row The Centre has directed ZEE5 to halt the release of the controversial docuseries Lawrence of Punjab amid political and public outcry over its portrayal of gangster culture.

King arrives on Christmas, makers of SRK's film confirm release date with teaser Shah Rukh Khan's mega release this year, also starring his daughter Suhana Khan, is set to hit the screens during Christmas. The makers dropped another teaser of the film on Friday.

Annu Kapoor on 'milky body' remark on Tamannaah: I'll touch her feet if she's hurt Annu Kapoor clarified that his 'milky body' comment on Tamannaah Bhatia was meant as appreciation of her performance. He expressed willingness to apologise and even touch her feet if she felt hurt by his remark.
SVC 63: Salman Khan's next with Nayanthara on Eid 2027 Salman Khan's upcoming film with Nayanthara is slated for Eid 2027 release. Directed by Vamshi Paidipally, the film is tentatively titled SVC63.

While it occasionally struggles to balance its message with storytelling, its raw honesty and relatable characters make it an engaging watch.

Sonam Kapoor shares a glimpse of newborn son with family pics: Life is a dream Sonam Kapoor shared fresh family photos featuring husband Anand Ahuja, son Vayu Ahuja and a glimpse of their newborn baby boy. The actor called this phase of her life "a dream".

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 36: Rs 1 crore on 5th Thursday as it enters 6th weekend Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues its record-breaking run as it enters week six, inching closer to the Rs 1,800 crore mark worldwide. Can the spy thriller surpass Baahubali 2: The Conclusion to become one of the highest-grossing Indian films ever?

Kajol explains breaking her no-kissing policy after 30 years in The Trial Kajol has finally opened up about breaking her long-standing no-kissing rule on screen in The Trial. She hinted that the choice was deeply tied to her character, who was opposite Jisshu Sengupta.

Remember Deewane To Deewane Hain singer Shweta Shetty? Here's what she is doing now Shweta Shetty, the 90s pop singer known for songs like Deewane Toh Deewane Hai and Dil Tote Tote Ho Gaya, spoke about her comeback plans. Read on to find out what she is doing now.

He also shared that the team spent nearly a year and a half researching Islamic law to ensure the film remained authentic.

Actor Trisha's voting post on Instagram sets off Vijay-linked chatter online The Tamil Nadu Assembly elections saw actor Vijay contesting with his TVK party for the first time. Actor Trisha's Instagram post after voting, featuring a song linked to Vijay, sparked social media speculation on political support.

Tamil actor Akshaya Hariharan claims someone voted in her name, seeks action Akshaya Hariharan claimed that someone else cast a vote in her name during the Tamil Nadu polls, calling the incident "shocking." Sharing a video, she urged voters to stay alert and said she plans to file a complaint.

Murder-charged singer D4vd had huge amount of child porn on iCloud: US prosecutors The 21-year-old singer, whose real name is David Burke, faces first-degree murder charges in the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, 14, whose dismembered remains were found in his Tesla in September last year.

Actor Amit Vikram on using comedy to address male harassment in Maamla Legal Hai 2 Actor Amit Vikram Pandey opens up about playing a male harassment survivor in Maamla Legal Hai Season 2 and why the story goes beyond a "man vs woman" debate.

general news.headline: Daughter was taken away by TMC: PM at rally with RG Kar rape victim's motherPM Narendra Modi, addressing a rally in Bengal with the mother of the RG Kar rape victim, accused the TMC of failing to protect women and silencing their demands for justice.

Why Israel is airlifting this 'lost tribe' from India's northeastFar from the Middle East, Israel undertook a silent mission in India. Announced last year by Benjamin Netanyahu, Israel on Thursday airlifted around 250 members of the Bnei Menashe community as part of its Operation Wings of Dawn.

Live: TMC has only looted Bengal in the past 15 years, says PM ModiAssembly Election Live Updates: With a record voter turnout in the first phase, West Bengal is now gearing up for the second phase of Assembly elections on April 29. With just days to go, the Trinamool Congress–BJP standoff has reached a peak, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah address rallies across the state today. The Trinamool camp is also intensifying its campaign, with Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee leading from the front. Meanwhile, post-poll discussions are gaining momentum in states that have already concluded voting, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Assam, and Puducherry. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Nirmala Sitharaman raises alarm on bank security risk due to Anthropic Mythos AIFinance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman held a high-level meeting with heads of banks on Thursday to discuss the potential risks with Anthropic's Claude Mythos, an AI so powerful that it can potentially hack any system. Sitharaman has advised for the creation of mechanisms that can identify and act on any cyberattacks on the financial sector.

How Babur used village homes as a shield in the Battle of PanipatIt has been 500 years since the first Battle of Panipat was fought, and it is remembered mainly for the first use of cannons in India, and the beginning of the Mughal empire. However, some literature suggests that the conquest was built not only on gunpowder and cavalry, but on civilians caught between his and Ibrahim Lodi's armies.

Meta, Oracle, and Microsoft lay off 46,750 employees in just one monthMeta, Oracle, and Microsoft have together impacted nearly 46,750 employees in just one month as the tech industry pivots sharply towards AI. While companies call it restructuring and investment in AI, thousands of workers are now facing the immediate reality of job loss and uncertainty.

We sail or sink together: NSA Ajit Doval meets eminent Indian MuslimsNational Security Adviser Ajit Doval invited 14 eminent people from the Muslim community to the new PMO building. Doval heard their concerns and suggestions, and spoke about the path for a stronger and united India.

20 scientists die or vanish across US, China: What's happening?The deaths of several defence-related scientists in the US and China in recent years raise questions due to the sensitivity of their fields. While no evidence of foul play has been found, the timing and circumstances have drawn public scrutiny.

One vote in Bengal, and Delhi-NCR is back to Covid chore daysCleaning, washing, cooking, the list is never-ending for residents of not just Delhi-NCR but beyond, who are having a hard time juggling work and home with their house help having gone to Bengal to cast their vote.

Pakistan's peace virus leads to lockdowns in twin cities, fuels public angerPakistan's bid to broker US-Iran peace talks is increasingly backfiring at home. Islamabad and Rawalpindi have remained under near-lockdown for days awaiting negotiations that have yet to materialise. This has crippled businesses, displaced workers and squeezed incomes, resulting in public anger.

India aa ke dekho: Iran slams Trump's hellhole remark, shares breathtaking videoIran's missions in India mocked Donald Trump after he shared remarks calling India a "hellhole," prompting strong backlash from New Delhi, which termed the comments "uninformed and inappropriate."

"I repeat, the files will be opened -- and this is Modi's guarantee," he said.

Gunman walks in calmly, then gunshots and panic: New video of Mexico firingWhile the gunman's presence did not initially raise any alarm, a commotion broke out when he supposedly started unpacking his bag. The tourists present there can be seen pacing down the steep ledges as gunshots ring out in the background.

Killer of IRS officer's daughter scored 90% in 12th, pawned marksheet to fund gamblingOnce a promising student, Rahul Meena's descent into online gambling and mounting debt culminated in the brutal murder of the IRS officer in Delhi.

Happy to see no violence: SC notes peaceful voting in Bengal, praises high turnoutThe Supreme Court commended West Bengal's record 92.88 per cent turnout in Phase 1 and praised the peaceful, violence-free conduct of polling.

Is Kolkata a city of slums like Amit Shah said? Here's the factAddressing a rally for the West Bengal election, Home Minister Amit Shah said Kolkata had turned into a city of slums under successive Communist and Trinamool Congress governments. Is it a fact that Kolkata, the capital of West Bengal, is a city of slums? Here's the fact.

This Indian physicist helped prove the Raman Effect, but got no NobelIndian physicist K.S. Krishnan spent months in a darkroom testing 65 liquids to prove what the world now calls the Raman Effect, yet C.V. Raman alone took the Nobel home.

Live: Israeli army blows up homes in southern Lebanon's Khiam, says reportUS-Iran War Live Updates: The situation in the Middle East remains tense as military activity and diplomatic efforts continue at the same time. The USS George HW Bush has arrived in the region, increasing the number of US aircraft carriers in operation there to three. Donald Trump has stated that any deal with Iran will be made only when it is "appropriate and good" for the United States. Iranian leaders, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, have rejected claims that there are divisions within the country's leadership. The US has taken further military steps. The US President has ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats suspected of placing mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The US military has also intensified mine-clearing operations in the waterway. In addition, the Pentagon said US forces boarded the tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean. These developments come after the US extended a ceasefire with Iran, although there is no formal extension or confirmed timeline for further talks. Diplomatic efforts are continuing alongside military developments. The United States is hosting a second round of talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington. The discussions are focused on extending a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Both sides have carried out strikes during the truce period. So far, there is no clear deadline for ending the conflict or for reaching a new agreement. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Early monsoon alert: Weather systems signal ahead-of-time arrival likely in IndiaFresh forecasts from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) suggest the southwest monsoon could make an early start, bringing much-needed relief from rising heat.

Didi is going: Amit Shah says BJP winning 110 of 152 Phase 1 seats in BengalAmit Shah said the surge in voter turnout in West Bengal signals support for the BJP, predicting Mamata Banerjee's exit and a saffron sweep with 110 of 152 seats.

Woman's mistake leaves toddler locked inside car with keys; video sparks outrageThe X account, which posted the video, claimed that the woman had left the child sitting inside the car and even handed him the keys. The child accidentally pressed the lock button, causing the car to lock from inside.

THE Asia Rankings 2026: 128 Indian universities ranked, none in top 40; China rulesThe Times Higher Education (THE) World and Asia Rankings 2026 are out. China continues to dominate the top positions in Asia, while the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bengaluru leads India as the top-performing university, ranked 43rd in Asia in computer science. India also has the highest representation in the rankings with 128 universities. Check India's top 10 universities and how they performed among Asia's best this year.

sports.headline: Vinod Kambli celebrates Sachin Tendulkar's birthday with kids at Shivaji Park Former India cricketer Vinod Kambli marked his childhood friend Sachin Tendulkar's birthday with a heartfelt gesture at Shivaji Park, where their cricketing journeys first took shape, as he shared ice cream with young aspiring cricketers.

Patidar has stuck to team's line of thinking: Mo Bobat on RCB's aggressive cricket IPL 2026: RCB are doubling down on their ultra-aggressive identity ahead of a key clash with Gujarat Titans, with Mo Bobat backing Rajat Patidar's calm leadership at the heart of it. Called it? Akeal Hosein's Jadeja-like performance resurfaces old tweet IPL 2026: Akeal Hosein's four-wicket haul for CSK against MI at the Wankhede brought back an old tweet where he had expressed admiration for Ravindra Jadeja, drawing striking comparisons. Chinnaswamy Stadium in a high-stakes clash between Shubman Gill's side with struggling middle order and Rajat Patidar's side with vulnerable death bowling. With the head-to-head record at this venue favoring the Titans, both teams are seeking a crucial batting resurgence in their final home league game of the season.

Mumbai's El Clasico nightmare, Chennai's delight at Wankhede: In Stats IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Chennai Super Kings dominated the El Clasico on Thursday, beating Mumbai Indians by 103 runs at the Wankhede Stadium. Concussion Sub for shoulder injury? MI explain debatable Mitchell Santner call IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Mumbai Indians' decision to replace Mitchell Santner with Shardul Thakur as a concussion substitute has raised questions over the injury's nature and whether the move complied with IPL rules on like-for-like replacements.

IPL Play of the Day: CSK went Plan B, then Akeal Hosein made Mumbai Indians pay IPL Play of the Day, MI vs CSK: Sanju Samson's century set the stage, but Chennai Super Kings' mid-innings pivot with the Impact Sub proved decisive. CSK share crucial injury updateIPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Michael Hussey said MS Dhoni is progressing well from a calf injury, batting strongly in nets and expected to return as a wicketkeeper once fully fit. Same track, same soil, should've batted better: Hardik Pandya after loss vs CSKMI vs CSK: Hardik Pandya admitted MI fell short in execution after a poor powerplay derailed their chase, insisting conditions were not to blame and that the team simply needed to bat better.

Samson, spin twins decimate MI as CSK win IPL El Clasico without ThalaIPL, MI vs CSK: Chennai Super Kings inflicted Mumbai Indians' biggest defeat by runs in IPL history, cruising to a dominant El Clásico win on the back of Sanju Samson's unbeaten century and Akeal Hosein's match-turning spell.

Sanju turns up vs arch-rivals, becomes CSK's joint-top centurion in just 7 gamesMI vs CSK: Sanju Samson silenced the Wankhede crowd with a masterclass in resilience and power-hitting.
